action: Add an explicit compatibility classifier for historical queue-update schema deviations without rewriting source history.
memory_recovered: Historical operational records must remain immutable evidence. Compatibility is a separate, explicit, narrow decision layer; it may permit deterministic processing but cannot convert malformed or ambiguous history into authoritative fact.

observed: Compatibility classifier, deterministic test contract, and evidence packet were committed additively
The inspected M-005 commit returned no associated workflow runs
inferred: Explicit exceptions reduce the risk of parser tolerance silently becoming historical truth
proposed: Gate effective-queue reconciliation on a corpus-wide compatibility report
superseded: Silently normalizing historical queue updates in place or accepting any parser-readable shape
unresolved: Corpus-wide execution and CI passage remain unobserved

privacy_boundary: Public repository metadata and abstract operational claims only; private chats, health and pet-health material, credentials, identifiers, and unreviewed uploads excluded.
verification: GitHub returned commit identities for each artifact. Runtime tests and CI execution were not available, so passage is not claimed.
next_action: Add corpus discovery to execute the compatibility classifier across every queue-update file, emit a compatibility report artifact, and block reconciliation on any unmanifested or invalid exception.
